name: Nao Finance
oneliner: Retail ELTIF 2.0 distributor bringing institutional PE, private debt, and infrastructure funds to European consumers from €1. Natixis and Muzinich partnerships live.
finserv: Distribution partnerships with Natixis / Muzinich private-debt ELTIF; operates under ELTIF 2.0; BaFin-adjacent through partners. Entry points from €1 or €1,000 savings plans.
team: Berlin-based; strategic tie-ups with tier-1 asset managers compensate for modest headline capital-raise visibility. Team details not widely public yet.
thesis: ELTIF 2.0 was the biggest European retail-access regulatory unlock of the last decade, and most traditional banks are moving far too slowly. Nao is the cleanest European bet that the private-markets wrapper will be the next 'ETF' retail moment — a textbook Caroline Federal inversion.
